isolation: Hardware-isolated microVM boundary (each sandbox fully separated from the host, the platform, and every other sandbox)
name: Azure Container Apps Sandboxes
notes: Sandboxes are a first-class resource type (Microsoft.App/SandboxGroups) in Container Apps, alongside apps, jobs, and dynamic sessions. They support standard OCI container images and include built-in suspend and resume capabilities.
summary: Azure Container Apps Sandboxes are a Microsoft-managed compute product that runs AI agent code in secure, ephemeral, hardware-isolated microVM environments. Each sandbox starts in sub-second time, scales to thousands, and costs nothing when idle. Developers create and manage sandboxes through the Azure Container Apps CLI or SDK and can run standard OCI container images inside them.
